title: Rival Nations Seize On Choke Points to Counter Trump
summary: From Iran to China, President Trump’s global aggression has encouraged other countries to search for new ways to pressure the U.S. economy.

title: Pam Bondi Fired as Trump’s Attorney General
summary: In a social media post, President Trump said he was replacing Ms. Bondi with Todd Blanche, her deputy, on an interim basis.

title: Who Is Todd Blanche, Trump’s Acting Attorney General?
summary: While it remains unclear how long Todd Blanche will stay in the job, whoever ends up taking over permanently will lead a department that he has shaped in his own image.

title: The Awe of a Moon Launch in an Age of Trump, Turmoil and Tribal Divisions
summary: The launch of Artemis II captured the tenor of the times in a country that can still do big things but seems forever mired in big problems.

title: Hegseth Fires Army Chief Amid Battle With Its Leaders
summary: Senior Army officers reacted with anger and frustration to news of Gen. Randy George’s dismissal, characterizing it as the latest blow to the service.

title: Planning Commission Approves Trump’s Ballroom, but Legal Roadblocks Remain
summary: The board had been expected to vote to approve the project last month, but it was delayed after about 32,000 mostly negative comments rolled in from across the country.

title: What to Know About the ‘Massive’ Military Bunker Beneath Trump’s Ballroom
summary: President Trump has been talking about the emergency facility beneath what was once the East Wing, details of which are usually kept secret, as he tries to justify his renovation.

title: What Drives Patrick Radden Keefe?
summary: Patrick Radden Keefe’s carefully applied ambition has propelled him to a rarefied perch.

title: Johnson Wavers on Ending the Shutdown, Reflecting His Weak Hold on Power
summary: The House speaker first panned, then endorsed, then punted on, then pitched and now is delaying a bill to reopen the Homeland Security Department, showing his vulnerability in the face of party rifts.

title: Tina Peters, Colorado Election Denier, Will Have Prison Sentence Reconsidered
summary: Ms. Peters, a former county clerk, received a nine-year sentence after being convicted of tampering with voting machines. An appeals court overturned the sentence but did not immediately free her from prison.

title: In the Birthright Citizenship Hearing, a Story of Asians Fighting for Rights
summary: In the Supreme Court’s oral arguments, lawyers and justices cited a litany of cases reflecting how long it took for Asians to win the right to be American.

title: In Supreme Court Birthright Citizenship Case, Trump’s Likely Loss May Not End the Fight
summary: The justices gave respectful consideration to what was once a fringe theory and could rule against it on grounds that would allow Congress to return to the question.

title: A President, the Supreme Court and a Landmark Citizenship Order Collide
summary: The justices seems poised to rule against the president’s birthright citizenship plan. He is already furious over their decision rejecting his tariffs program.

title: Trump’s Media-Bashing Is Coming Back to Bite Him in Court
summary: Judges have cited attacks on the press by the president and his appointees when ruling against the government in at least three court cases.

title: Investigators Said to Look at Whether LaGuardia Controller Stepped Away
summary: Officials are said to be examining whether a controller had to use an emergency phone just before the crash at LaGuardia. They have yet to determine the cause of the crash and are also focusing on other avenues.

title: Why People With Chronic Illness Are Turning to AI Chatbots for Health Advice
summary: Some women with complex chronic illnesses are using chatbots to search for diagnoses or relief from their symptoms.

title: OpenAI Buys Streaming Show ‘TBPN,’ Aiming to Change Narrative on A.I.
summary: OpenAI said the deal would help it “create a space for a real, constructive conversation about the changes A.I. creates.”

title: Evacuated From War-Torn Gaza, 11 Toddlers Return Home to Hugs and Tears
summary: The Palestinian children were born prematurely in the early days of the war and moved from a besieged hospital to Egypt. They have now returned home to the enclave, which lies in ruins.

title: Blake Lively’s Harassment Claims Against Justin Baldoni Dismissed as Case Heads to Trial
summary: A federal judge tossed the actress’s sexual harassment claims against Justin Baldoni in a split decision that sets the stage for a contentious trial.

title: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson on Marriage, Secrets and ‘The Drama’
summary: The stars of the new film, “The Drama,” debate the pros and cons of dredging up the past.

title: ‘The Drama’ Review: Her Secret Is His Crisis
summary: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson play a couple upended when one confesses to coming close to committing a horrific crime.

title: The One Thing Trump Wanted That Pam Bondi Failed to Deliver
summary: The core of Mr. Trump’s dissatisfaction with the attorney general was apparently her failure to serve his need for revenge against his enemies.

title: Every President Tries It. It Never Works.
summary: What’s really behind all these attempts to revive American industry?

title: Trump Fires His Attorney General
summary: Also, France’s president voices European frustration with the U.S. leader. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.

title: How Are High California Gas Prices Affecting Your Life?
summary: Tell us how the sharp increase in gas prices is changing how you live and work.

title: Biruté Galdikas, 79, Who Worked to Save Wild Orangutans in Borneo, Dies
summary: With Jane Goodall and Dian Fossey, she was one of three prominent researchers of great apes who were sometimes called the “trimates.”

title: Hershey’s Promises to Use Only Real Chocolate After Backlash
summary: The change follows weeks of complaints from a grandson of the Reese’s Peanut Butter Cup inventor and other vocal consumers.

title: Gucci Mane Held at Gunpoint, Prompting 2 Rappers’ Arrests
summary: The hip-hop titan was forced to sign papers releasing a rapper known as Pooh Shiesty from his record contract during an armed confrontation in January at a Dallas music studio, officials said.

title: Danish Warship Sunk by Britain’s Lord Nelson 225 Years Ago Is Found
summary: The ship sank during the Battle of Copenhagen, an important moment in Danish and British history, and became the origin of a common saying.
